statement: Nvidia reported first-quarter revenue of $81.62 billion, beating analyst estimates of $79.18 billion.
Nvidia's data center revenue rose 92% year over year in the first quarter.
Nvidia posted adjusted earnings of $1.87 per share, beating estimates of $1.77.
Nvidia's growth was driven by surging data center demand.
Apple reported second-quarter revenue of $111.2 billion, above estimates of $109.66 billion.
Apple's Greater China revenue was $20.50 billion in the second quarter.
Apple posted adjusted earnings per share of $2.01, beating estimates of $1.96.
Apple's iPhone revenue was $56.99 billion in the second quarter.
Apple's revenue beat was driven by strong iPhone and China sales.
Microsoft reported adjusted earnings per share of $4.27, beating consensus by $0.21.
Microsoft reported revenue of $82.89 billion, beating estimates by about $1.5 billion.
Microsoft's revenue grew because of strong Azure and cloud demand.
Amazon reported earnings per share of $2.78, beating estimates by $1.10.
Amazon reported revenue of $181.5 billion, beating estimates by over $4 billion.
Amazon Web Services revenue rose 24% to $35.6 billion in the fourth quarter of 2025.
Alphabet reported adjusted earnings per share of $2.62, missing estimates by a penny.
Alphabet reported revenue of nearly $110 billion, beating estimates by $2.7 billion.
Google Cloud revenue soared 63% year over year.
Tesla posted first-quarter revenue of $22.39 billion, topping the consensus estimate of $21.92 billion.
Tesla reported first-quarter adjusted earnings of 41 cents per share.
Tesla's services and other revenue climbed to $3.75 billion from $2.64 billion a year earlier.
Meta's first-quarter revenue beat was driven by strength in advertising.
Meta's CFO said the company will fund its AI ambitions primarily with cash rather than debt.
JPMorgan generated fourth-quarter net income of $14.7 billion excluding a significant item.
JPMorgan reported fourth-quarter net revenue of $46.8 billion, up 7%.
JPMorgan reported net interest income of $25.1 billion in the fourth quarter, up 7%.
U.S. consumer prices rose 3.8% over the 12 months ending in April 2026, the fastest annual pace since May 2023.
The 12-month energy index gain reached 17.9% in the April 2026 inflation report.
U.S. GDP grew at a 2.1% pace in 2025.
Energy costs tied to the war with Iran drove U.S. inflation higher in April 2026.
Brent crude was trading around $105 a barrel, up 44% since before the Iran war started.
Oil prices rose because the Iran war tightened global crude supply.
The S&P 500 closed at a record 7,501.24 on May 15, 2026.
The S&P 500 closed above the 7,500 mark for the first time in its history.
The S&P 500 was on track for its longest weekly winning streak since 2023.
EUR/USD was trading at about 1.1637, down slightly on the day.
Gold was trading at $4,879.60 an ounce, up 1.48% on the day.
Copper was trading around $6.11 per pound.
Natural gas was trading around $2.67 per million BTU.
The Dow Jones Industrial Average closed at 50,579.70, up 0.58%.
The Nasdaq Composite closed at 26,343.97, up 0.19%.
The 10-year Treasury yield was about 4.26%.
The 10-year Treasury yield rose to its highest level in 10 months on hotter-than-expected inflation.
The U.S. added 115,000 nonfarm payrolls in April 2026.
The U.S. unemployment rate was unchanged at 4.3% in April 2026.
The U.S. lost 92,000 nonfarm payrolls in February 2026, with unemployment climbing to 4.4%.
Stronger-than-expected jobs data pushed back expectations for Fed rate cuts.
USD/JPY was trading at about 159.24.
GBP/JPY was trading at about 214.82.
Bitcoin was trading at about $81,240, up 0.35% over 24 hours.
